The '''Ciutric Hegemony''' was a wealthy region in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], consisting of 25 worlds dominated by [[Ciutric IV]]. The Ciutric Hegemony also designated the aristocratic regime that controlled the region during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] and the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]].

During the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Ciutric Hegemony was ruled by [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] who turned the region into a model Imperial state. After the death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Ciutric Hegemony fell to [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] [[Delak Krennel]], who oppressively ruled over the region as [[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]]. The Hegemony became a stronghold of Imperial power and one of the numerous Imperial splinter factions targeted by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in the decade following the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Ciutric, which is the political and economic hub of the Hegemony.|[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]]|<ref name="IR" />}}
The Ciutric Hegemony was a cluster of 25 wealthy worlds,<ref name="TNEGtC" /> located in 12 [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA" /> The Hegemony was politically and economically dominated by [[Ciutric IV]], in the [[Ciutric system/Legends|Ciutric system]]. Other star systems included the [[Liinade system/Legends|Liinade]] and the [[Corvis Minor system/Legends|Corvis Minor systems]]<ref name="IR" /> in the [[Ciutric sector/Legends|Ciutric sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Although the [[Ciutric sector/Legends|Ciutric sector]] formed the core of the Hegemony, the latter also encompassed [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] located in neighboring [[Sector/Legends|sectors]]<ref name="TEA" /><ref name="EAOC" /> such as the [[Axxila system/Legends|Axxila system]]<ref name="TEGtW" /> in the [[D'Astan sector/Legends|D'Astan sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> and the [[Vrosynri system/Legends|Vrosynri system]]<ref name="IR" /> in the [[Nijune sector/Legends|Nijune sector]].<ref name="EAOC" />

==History==
{{Quote|He murdered Sate Pestage and took over his holdings-the Ciutric Hegemony. It made him the leader of a dozen worlds and gave him a fair amount of material resources. He didn't join Thrawn per se, but seems to have given him monetary support. He rules from Ciutric and has a fleet of a dozen or so capital ships, including his Reckoning.|Admiral Ackbar, about [[Delak Krennel]]|<ref name="IR" />}}
The Ciutric Hegemony was an aristocratic state<ref name="FaDCR" /> controlled by corporate interests.<ref name="TEGtW" /> It had been ruled for generations by the [[Pestage family]].<ref name="FaDCR" /> During the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the Hegemony was defended by the [[Ciutric Planetary Security Forces]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Ciutric Hegemony joined the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], prompting Republic loyalist such as the [[Free Axxilans]] to relocate to neighboring systems. The Hegemony remained in Separatist space until the end of the Clone Wars. In the aftermath of the war, the Separatists were driven out of the region by the newly-formed [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Ciutric Offensive]]. [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Pensar Luc]] became the Imperial [[Governor/Legends|governor]] of the Ciutric Hegemony. The Moff created the [[Axxila antipirate fleet]] from veterans of the Ciutric Planetary Security Forces to pacify the region.<ref name="TEGtW" /> The Ciutric Hegemony was eventually given to [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]], [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] of the Galactic Empire by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], the Hegemony became a model Imperial state.<ref name="FaDCR" />

[[File:Empire_7-8_ABY.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Ciutric Hegemony by 7 ABY]]
Sate Pestage was [[Evacuation of Ciutric|assassinated]] on Ciutric IV by Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Delak Krennel]] several months after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="XWRS35">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 35|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 35]]</ref> The Ciutric Hegemony was soon usurped by the murderer of Pestage who made the region into his pocket Empire and turned the Hegemony into an Imperial stronghold. As the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] battered the remnants of the Empire, the self-styled [[Prince-Admiral/Legends|Prince-Admiral]] turned the Hegemony into a sanctuary for supporters of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Althoug the Hegemony's population was only 56% [[Human/Legends|Human]], with several worlds having a large alien majority, Krennel operated it as a [[Humanocentrism|humanocentrist]] regime. The Prince-Admiral maintained his power by frequent, bloody purges of any possible opposition, including the Pestage family.<ref name="IR" /> Krennel also used barbarous methods to expand his influence to nearby sectors, overrunning other Imperial warlords such as [[Gaen Drommel]] and [[Walang Grazz]].<ref name="Despoilers">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-1|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>

[[File:BattleofLiinadeIII.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] [[Battle of Liinade III|attacked]] Ciutric Hegemony forces on [[Liinade III]]]]
In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the Ciutric Hegemony [[Thrawn's confederation|supported]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] during his [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] against the New Republic.<ref name="TEA" /> After the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|defeat]] of Thrawn later the same year, the New Republic sought to eliminate the remaining Imperial warlords. Being the most powerful at that moment, Krennel was first on the list.<ref name="Despoilers" /> In response, the Prince-Admiral made some peace overtures to the New Republic but used the media to spin-doctor xenophobic ideas into a political tool against the New Republic. Moreover, the warlord attempted to develop a new [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]: the [[Pulsar Station]]. As the confrontation proved inevitable, [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] launched a [[Hegemony theater|series of raids]] against the Hegemony. Finally, the forces of the Ciutric Hegemony were defeated during the [[Battle of Ciutric]]; the the Prince-Admiral was killed in the battle.<ref name="IR" />

After the [[Imperial Reunification]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], the Ciutric Hegemony remained in [[Imperial Remnant]] [[Imperial Space|space]] until [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the region was [[Attack on the Ciutric system|invaded]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], and several of its star systems, including the Ciutric and Axxila systems, were conquered by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="TEA" />
